evidence: All 80 milestone_record tests pass (0 failures, 0 ignored)
cargo clippy -- -D warnings passes cleanly (no warnings)

Amendment fr-8-841107a4 (redundant active_bead_ids): service.rs:171-175 now computes active_beads as a direct filter on current_bead_runs (already output of latest_task_runs_per_bead at line 169) instead of calling active_bead_ids() which would redundantly call latest_task_runs_per_bead internally. The redundant O(n) pass and misleading indirection are eliminated.

Amendment fr-8-7e4d3238 (single-use LockAttemptSnapshotStore): service.rs:719-721 adds doc comment 'Single-use test helper that coordinates exactly one contended lock acquisition.' Lines 767-768 and 777-778 use .expect('LockAttemptSnapshotStore is single-use') instead of silent no-op on .take(), so a second use will panic rather than silently skip coordination.

Amendment fr-8-beaac5f9 (duplicate journal events on backfill): fs.rs:2417-2438 adds repairable_completion_event() which detects same-bead, same-timestamp, same-event-type completion events with mergeable details. fs.rs:2479-2491 in append_event_if_missing now tries repair-merge before appending, updating the existing event in-place via write_journal(). CompletionJournalDetails::merge (fs.rs:2317-2345) handles field-level merging with conflict detection.

Amendment fr-8-beaac5f9 verified by test: completion_replay_backfills_existing_journal_event_without_duplicate (service.rs:2242-2328) calls update_task_run without plan_hash/outcome_detail, then replays with plan_hash='plan-v2' and outcome_detail='backfilled detail'; asserts exactly 1 BeadCompleted journal event with merged details.

Amendment fr-8-3e84cce1 (Paused state clobbered): service.rs:217-223 now checks `snapshot.status != MilestoneStatus::Paused` in the condition that would rewrite to Ready, so Paused milestones retain their status during lineage reconciliation.

Amendment fr-8-3e84cce1 verified by test: paused_milestone_stays_paused_when_completion_reconciles_lineage (service.rs:1090-1158) starts a bead, sets milestone to Paused, completes the bead, and asserts snapshot.status == MilestoneStatus::Paused with active_bead=None and completed_beads=1.

model.rs: TaskRunEntry retains all required fields — milestone_id, bead_id, project_id, run_id, plan_hash, outcome, outcome_detail, started_at, finished_at — with serde(default, skip_serializing_if) for backward compatibility

service.rs: reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage (163-226) rebuilds counters from latest_task_runs_per_bead, preserves Paused, transitions Active→Ready when no active beads remain, validates terminal invariants
service.rs: All mutation paths (update_status, persist_plan, record_bead_start, update_task_run) run under with_milestone_write_lock for serialized access

All prior acceptance criteria remain satisfied: milestone state can answer 'what happened to bead X?', controller can update linkage after each run outcome, all new fields are optional for backward compatibility
findings_or_gaps: AppError::RunStartFailed is still used (service.rs:555) for rejecting non-terminal outcomes in update_task_run, which is semantically a finalization rejection. A more specific error variant would be cleaner but this is cosmetic, not a correctness issue — carried forward from prior reviews.

The outstanding risk — milestone mutations span separate lineage, snapshot, and journal files without a cross-file transaction — remains. All paths are serialized by the milestone mutation lock, replay-safe, and the new journal repair mechanism further strengthens consistency. This is an acceptable trade-off for file-based storage.

The active_bead_ids function (model.rs:368-374) still exists with its internal latest_task_runs_per_bead call. It is no longer called from reconcile_snapshot_from_lineage but remains available for external callers. If callers pass already-collapsed data, the redundancy remains — but this is outside the scope of the current fix and the function's contract is self-contained.
